task_completion.description: Fix the bug causing random file dropping in the documentation builder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing modifications to the file traversal, reading, or filtering logic within the Node.js scripts at /home/ga/workspace/docs_builder/ to ensure all markdown files are processed.

task_completion.description: Fix the bug causing content loss after horizontal rules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing modifications to the markdown parsing logic (e.g., regex handling '---' or '***' or split functions) to prevent the parser from truncating or ignoring content after a horizontal rule.

task_completion.description: Fix the incorrect parsing of multiple wiki-links on a single line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing updates to the wiki-link parsing regex (e.g., adding a global '/g' flag or fixing greedy matching) for links formatted like '[[link]]'.

task_completion.description: Fix false dead link reports for files with spaces.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing the addition of URL decoding (e.g., decodeURI, decodeURIComponent) or proper string escaping in the link validation logic.

task_completion.description: Fix broken relative paths for nested assets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing corrections to path resolution logic (e.g., using path.relative, path.join, or adjusting directory traversal levels) for images or other assets.

task_completion.description: Successfully execute 'npm run build' to generate the HTML corpus.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window showing the command 'npm run build' being executed in /home/ga/workspace/docs_builder/ and completing successfully without errors, specifically showing no dropped files or false dead link reports.

integrity.description: The agent modified the Node.js builder scripts rather than manually editing the generated HTML files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent editing .js files in the workspace. There should be no evidence of the agent manually typing out HTML content to bypass the builder.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely ran the build command to test and generate the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history shows 'npm run build' or 'node <script>' actually running and producing standard output, rather than the agent just echoing a fake success message.

integrity.description: The agent fixed the existing builder rather than replacing it with a trivial mock script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code editor should show the original complex builder script with specific targeted bug fixes, not a completely rewritten script that just prints 'Build successful'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires fixing 5 specific bugs in a Node.js documentation builder located at /home/ga/workspace/docs_builder/. The pass threshold is 60 points. The VLM should look for code edits addressing: file dropping (likely an async/await or globbing issue), horizontal rule truncation (likely a bad regex or string split), wiki-link regex (likely missing a global 'g' flag), URL encoding/decoding for spaces in dead link checks, and relative path resolution for nested assets. Finally, it must verify 'npm run build' executes successfully without the aforementioned errors.
